title: Platform-native runtime: interactive sessions + subagent-per-task, generalist workers
context: claude -p moves to API-rate billing; the v1 Python harness re-implemented platform capabilities (sessions, worktrees, hooks) and fought them. Needed an execution model that runs on subscription billing with zero TUI automation.

options.name: repaired-python-harness
options.rationale: Keep the supervisor spawning claude -p per task; fix isolation, substrate, quota gaps.
options.tradeoff: API billing once -p policy lands; permanent maintenance of plumbing the platform is absorbing.

options.name: platform-native-with-tmux-watchdog
options.rationale: Interactive sessions driven by a watchdog typing /clear and kickoff prompts via tmux send-keys.
options.tradeoff: TUI automation is brittle across Claude Code updates; deeper policy gray zone.

options.name: platform-native-no-automation
options.rationale: Engine = file contracts + skills + deterministic sb CLI. Workers are hand-started interactive sessions running a self-pacing /sb-work loop; each task executes in a fresh subagent with a per-task model override; planning and verification are task types.
options.tradeoff: No supervisor: wedged sessions surface via stale-heartbeat notification, not instant restart; subagent budgets enforced softly (hooks/loop checks, not process kills).

reasoning: Subagent-per-task preserves HDR-001's real commitment (fresh CONTEXT per task) inside long-running sessions — PHI-012's validated pattern. Tier moves from session to dispatch: generalist workers, better quota utilization, and one session can run the whole pipeline (the self-hosting MVP shape). Zero claude -p and zero API calls keeps the fleet on subscription billing. Supersedes HDR-001's tier-pinned-pool mechanics while preserving its intent; research handoffs use spawn-and-release continuations with enqueue-time DAG cycle checks and max chain depth 3, so waiting parents never hold workers and deadlock cannot occur.
